pattern: Dividing the value axis into buckets of width valueDiff+1 means any two numbers in the same bucket are close enough, and only the two neighbouring buckets can also be close; keeping at most one number per bucket over a sliding window of indexDiff makes each check constant time.
hints: Two conditions must hold at once: the indices are within indexDiff and the values are within valueDiff; handle the index part with a sliding window and the value part with bucketing.
If you floor each value into a bucket of width valueDiff+1, two numbers sharing a bucket are guaranteed within valueDiff, and you only need to also check the immediately adjacent buckets.

Slide a window of the last indexDiff entries, storing each value by bucket; on insert, a collision in the same bucket means success, and check the left and right neighbour buckets for a value within valueDiff before evicting the entry that leaves the window.

edge_inputs.description: equal values within index distance, valueDiff zero
values too far apart in value for the given window
close values one apart in index and value
valueDiff satisfied but indices too far apart
huge valueDiff makes any in-window pair qualify
off-by-one in value gap should fail

stress.description: moderate array, small value range and small diffs so brute oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    indexDiff = rng.randint(1, 15)
    valueDiff = rng.randint(0, 10)
    return [nums, indexDiff, valueDiff]
stress.note: small ranges create frequent qualifying and disqualifying pairs near the boundaries
